equivalence_argument: No complete mapping: HTTP executors are accepted, but LLM-only executors are rejected by exact capability equality.

checked_queries: persistent agent HttpCall Llm capability validation
negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Persistent-agent executor admission accepts only the metadata expected capability tag, which is HttpCall, and has no alternate Llm acceptance path.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/rpc/rpc/src/async_tx_validator.rs validate_executor compares executor.capability for exact equality with the single expected tag.
Capability::Llm exists, but no persistent-agent branch accepts either HttpCall or Llm.

notes: The metadata comment says the current downstream parity route is HttpCall, not an either-or capability set.
rationale: The commit imports PERSISTENT_AGENT_PRECOMPILE into the RPC async validator and maps it to vec![Capability::HttpCall, Capability::Llm] with a comment that it is routed through executor-go HTTP handler like sovereign agent. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Executor registration validation for PERSISTENT_AGENT_PRECOMPILE must accept executors with either HttpCall or Llm capability, matching sovereign-agent routing.
suggested_falsification_tests: Validate persistent-agent requests against registry fixtures containing only HttpCall, only Llm, and neither capability; assert the first two pass capability validation and the last fails.
summary: Persistent-agent validation does not accept LLM-only executors.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Validate persistent-agent admission with registry fixtures for HttpCall-only, Llm-only, and neither; current code only accepts HttpCall-only.
title: Persistent agent uses HTTP or LLM executors
